turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've had a persistent cough for about five days now, and it just won't go away. It started dry, but over the past few days, it's become really productive with yellowish-green sputum. I've tried taking dextromethorphan to suppress it, but it hasn't helped much.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: My cough is my main issue - it's persistent and doesn't seem to be getting better with over-the-counter medication. It's really frustrating me because I'm an elementary school teacher, and I need to be able to breathe and teach without being miserable.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about 5 days ago. I was feeling fine one day, and then just woke up with a dry cough that I couldn't shake.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens throughout the day, but especially at night when I'm trying to sleep - it really interrupts my rest.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's about an 8 or 9 out of 10. The fact that it's persistent and disrupting my daily life, including my sleep, makes it really bothersome.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: Yes, when I cough up sputum, it's accompanied by some difficulty breathing and wheezing. It's not severe, but it's definitely noticeable and uncomfortable.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: It gets a lot worse when I'm exerting myself or trying to speak loudly - like in class when I have to yell over the kids. It feels like my lungs are burning and making it hard for me to catch my breath.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: I've been experiencing night sweats - every night for the past 4 nights, I wake up with pajamas soaked through. It's really concerning me because I'm not sure what's causing it and it's making me feel even more unwell than my cough already is.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: I've been noticing a sharp right-sided chest pain that gets worse with deep breathing and coughing. It's like a stabbing sensation, especially when I inhale deeply or cough, which is making me even more anxious about my condition.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: I don't think so, but to be honest, I'm not really sure. I haven't noticed any swelling in my legs or feet, and it hasn't been a concern for me until now that we're talking about all these other symptoms. To be frank, I'm more worried about this cough and the night sweats than anything else right now.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I've been taking cetirizine 10mg as needed during allergy season because I have seasonal allergies, but that's not really helping with this cough. And for the past week, I've also been taking over-the-counter dextromethorphan to try and suppress it, like I mentioned earlier.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: To be honest, I'm not sure. I don't think so? I've had a physical exam about a year ago, everything was normal, but that's about it. I do take cetirizine for my allergies and have a penicillin allergy, which causes a rash, but I'm not aware of any other medical conditions.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: I don't think so? I mean, I've been pretty healthy my whole life, and like I said, everything was normal at my physical exam a year ago. I'm not really sure about cholesterol levels, to be honest. Can we focus on this cough and what's going on with me right now? I'm getting a bit anxious about all these other symptoms and just want to get to the bottom of it.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I do have an occasional glass of wine on weekends, maybe one or two glasses. But that's really not a regular thing for me.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: To be honest, I'm not sure how relevant this is to my symptoms right now. I'm more concerned about getting to the bottom of what's causing this cough and the night sweats. As an elementary school teacher, my diet is pretty standard - I eat a lot of sandwiches for lunch and grab coffee or smoothies in the morning. But if you're asking about exercise habits, I try to stay active by walking with my partner on weekends, but lately, I've been feeling too tired from this cough to do much of anything strenuous.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: My mom has asthma, which has been a concern for her over the years. She's always been really proactive about managing it with medication and lifestyle changes. I'm not sure if that's related to my symptoms, but I thought I'd mention it since you asked about family history.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My mom has asthma, which is something she's managed pretty well over the years. That's actually one of my biggest concerns - I've been wondering if there's a connection between her asthma and my current symptoms.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: My last physical exam was about a year ago, and everything came back normal.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: Yes, I do. I'm allergic to penicillin - it causes a rash whenever I take it. That's something my doctor has known for a while now.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: Actually, yes. A few of my students in class have been out sick with upper respiratory infections lately, and it's got me wondering if this could be contagious or related to something we've all been exposed to at school. But to be honest, I'm not really sure how that would affect my symptoms or treatment.
